title: user — permissions
scope_suffix_convention: Every read + list permission may be seeded WITH a scope suffix (`.any`, `.tenant`, `.branch`, `.team`, `.own`). The base permission (no suffix) is the highest-tier variant, effectively `.tenant`. The ScopeSuffixResolver (shipped by this module, consumed by every base repository) applies the corresponding WHERE clause at query build time. See design.md §9 for the pattern.

permissions.name: users.viewAny.own
permissions.description: Callers see only themselves. Enables `/me`-adjacent lists (e.g. own memberships).

permissions.name: users.update.own
permissions.description: Enables `/me` self-updates on the non-PII fields.

permissions.name: users.erase
permissions.description: GDPR-triggered erasure. Higher bar than delete — only owner-tier by default.

permissions.name: profiles.view
permissions.description: Read a Profile (redacted by default). Every authenticated caller can read profiles — the redactor gates the PII cluster.

permissions.name: profile.view.pii
permissions.description: Unredacted read of first_name / last_name / phone_e164 / dob. Adult subjects.

permissions.name: profile.view.pii.child
permissions.description: Unredacted read of minor Profile PII. Guardians have this via their AthleteGuardian link; admins carry it explicitly.

permissions.name: profile.update.pii
permissions.description: Non-subject writes to first_name / last_name / dob / phone_e164.

permissions.name: tenant_members.viewAny.own
permissions.description: `/me/tenants` — caller sees only their own memberships.

permissions.name: tenant_members.switch
permissions.description: Any authenticated User can switch between their own memberships.

permissions.name: platform.profile.view.pii
permissions.description: Support-tier only carries redacted profile read.
